children.name: Menura
children.description: Lyrebirds — ancient Australian passerines renowned as the world's foremost avian mimics. The male's extraordinary tail, shaped like a Greek lyre when fanned, is the most elaborate of any bird. They can imitate any sound — from other birds' songs to chainsaws, camera shutters, and car alarms — with astonishing fidelity. Despite their superb mimicry, lyrebirds are rarely seen, spending most of their time scratching through forest leaf litter for invertebrates.
